name: Jamaican Jerked Pork Tenderloins
recipeInstructions:
1. Choose pork tenderloins that are at least 3/4 lb each. Jamaican-style, this dish can also be prepared with chicken breasts or legs.
2. If you prefer spicy food, increase the amount of hot pepper.
3. If fresh hot peppers are unavailable, use 1/2 tsp hot pepper sauce or reduce the amount to suit your family's taste.
4. Trim the pork tenderloins and place them in a shallow glass dish. Set aside.
5. In a food processor, pure onions, garlic, hot pepper, soy sauce, oil, orange rind, lemon rind, orange juice, ginger, dried thyme, cinnamon, ground allspice, nutmeg, curry powder, salt, and pepper.
6. Pour the onion mixture over the pork tenderloins, turning to coat all sides evenly.
7. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or up to 8 hours, turning the pork occasionally.
8. Remove the marinade and transfer the pork to a foil-lined rimmed baking sheet.
9. Broil, basting once with the marinade, for about 20 minutes or until the meat is browned but still has a hint of pink inside.
10. Tent with foil; let stand for 10 minutes before serving.
